metrics.name: trend
metrics.interpretation: Recent trajectory is positive or stable.

metrics.name: stability
metrics.interpretation: Score behavior is stable enough for higher-confidence recommendations.

metrics.name: sample_size
metrics.interpretation: Enough history to start model calibration seriously.

metrics.name: outcome_hit_rate
metrics.interpretation: Outcome learning exists, but recommendation quality still needs proof.

metrics.name: regret_average
metrics.interpretation: Regret remains high enough that certainty should stay capped.

strengths: History depth is sufficient to begin recommendation grading and regime detection.
weaknesses: Even naive next-step prediction remains noisy; forecast confidence should stay modest.
Outcome learning is still too weak to justify aggressive autonomy.
Average regret is still elevated; IGNIS should prefer reversible actions.
recommendations: Log prediction outcomes and recommendation acceptance on every scoring run.
Demote certainty automatically when stability and sample size are weak.
Track strategy-level hit rates, not just raw score accuracy.
Let regret and contradiction pressure cap execution autonomy and narrator assertiveness.
evaluationSummary: Stability 87 · hit rate 25 · regret 0.62 · confidence ×0.75 · Platt ECE 0.006

plattCalibration.summary: Platt calibration (n=12): ECE 0.006 · Brier 0.179 · quality=strong. System is overconfident — calibrated scores are lower than reported. A=-0.850, B=-0.441.
